name: Missing Value Fill (LOCF)
shortDescription: Carry each participant's last valid measurement forward across timepoints, with per-column validity ranges.
description: Fills gaps in a timepointed longitudinal table using last-observation-carried-forward, independently per participant and in timepoint order. Each column declares the range(s) its values may legitimately fall in; anything missing or out of range is treated as absent and replaced by that participant's most recent valid value. Visits the participant never attended are left blank rather than invented. Reports what was filled so the imputation is auditable.
inputDescription: A timepointed table, one row per participant per timepoint — typically the output of Visit Timepoint Assignment.

name.label: Name
name.description: Unique identifier for this node in the pipeline

parameters.label: Columns to fill
parameters.description: Map of column name to the range(s) its values may validly take. Values outside every range are treated as missing. Shorthand: a bare list of ranges.

participant_column.label: Participant ID column
participant_column.description: Values are only ever carried forward within one participant.

order_column.label: Timepoint order column
order_column.description: Numeric column giving each row's position in the sequence. Rows are sorted by this before filling.

attendance_column.label: Attendance column
attendance_column.description: Blank in this column means the participant did not attend, so the row is left empty rather than filled. Leave unset to treat every row as attended.

max_carry.label: Maximum consecutive fills
max_carry.description: How many timepoints in a row a single value may be carried. Leave blank for unlimited, as the original analysis did.

emit_full_grid.label: Emit a row for every timepoint
emit_full_grid.description: Pad each participant to one row per timepoint, blank where they did not attend, giving a balanced panel.

timepoint_column.label: Timepoint label column
timepoint_column.description: Label column written on padded rows, e.g. T1. Only used when emitting the full grid.

outputDescription: The filled table, plus a per-column report of how many values were imputed.
